title: Obsidian vault storage layer (vault-canonical + derived FactStore index)
description: Add a domain-agnostic vault storage layer to agent-bober where each per-domain repo is an Obsidian vault: markdown + YAML frontmatter is the canonical source of truth and FactStore (SQLite) is a derived, rebuildable index over note frontmatter. Provides generic note read/write/frontmatter helpers, a `bober vault reindex` command that parses frontmatter into FactStore reusing the existing reconcile-at-ingest path, an on-device-only Obsidian MCP read/write adapter, and a profile.yaml hook for later SOPS wiring.

clarificationQuestions.question: How should generic note frontmatter map onto the FactStore (scope/subject/predicate/value) so the derived index is domain-agnostic?

options.label: A
options.description: scope = caller-supplied domain; subject = frontmatter `id` if present else vault-relative note path; predicate = each frontmatter key; value = stringified frontmatter value

options.label: B
options.description: One fact per note with the whole frontmatter JSON-stringified into value (opaque blob)

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — matches FactStore's (scope, subject, predicate, value) signature (src/state/facts.ts:22-31) and lets reconcileFact dedup/supersede per-field at ingest (src/orchestrator/memory/reconcile.ts:51-101). B defeats per-field trend math the research doc requires (§3b line 151).

clarificationQuestions.question: Which Obsidian MCP server should the adapter target, given the layer must stay on-device only?

options.label: A
options.description: Server-agnostic: declare the MCP server in config (mcpCommand/mcpArgs/mcpEnv) mirroring ObservabilityProvider, with a configurable tool-name mapping and documented defaults for cyanheads/obsidian-mcp-server

options.label: B
options.description: Hardcode the Obsidian Local REST API plugin HTTP endpoint at 127.0.0.1:27124

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — research §2 (lines 49-50) lists multiple mature community servers; the ExternalMcpServer stdio pattern (src/mcp/external-client.ts) already spawns server-agnostic MCP subprocesses. Hardcoding one server couples the layer to one vendor and one transport.

clarificationQuestions.question: How should a note carrying frontmatter `status: superseded` affect the derived FactStore index during reindex?

options.label: A
options.description: Superseded notes contribute NO active facts; their frontmatter is skipped so only active notes' fields remain queryable (git history remains the bitemporal source)

options.label: B
options.description: Superseded notes are inserted then immediately invalidated via supersedeFact

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — research §3b (lines 152-156) locks git history as the bitemporal source and FactStore as a rebuildable active-state index; skipping superseded notes keeps reindex a pure rebuild of active state.

clarificationQuestions.question: Should the YAML frontmatter parser be a new dependency or a dependency-free implementation?

options.label: A
options.description: Generator's choice between a vetted YAML dependency and a minimal dependency-free parser, provided the documented Dataview frontmatter conventions (string/number/ISO-date/list/status) round-trip losslessly under test

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — library selection is a generator-implementation decision per planner discipline; the round-trip success criteria pin the observable behavior regardless of approach. Repo currently has no YAML dep (package.json lines 64-75), only sax.

resolvedClarifications.answer: Option A — scope = caller-supplied domain; subject = frontmatter `id` if present else vault-relative note path; predicate = frontmatter key; value = stringified frontmatter value. Cited: src/state/facts.ts:22-31 (FactSchema signature), src/orchestrator/memory/reconcile.ts:51-101 (per-key reconcile).

Option A — server-agnostic config-declared MCP server with configurable tool-name mapping (defaults for cyanheads/obsidian-mcp-server). Cited: src/mcp/external-client.ts:42-103 (stdio spawn + listTools/callTool), src/config/schema.ts:298-309 (ObservabilityProvider declaration shape), research §2 lines 49-50.

Option A — notes with frontmatter `status: superseded` contribute no active facts during reindex. Cited: research §3b lines 152-156 (git history is the bitemporal source; FactStore is the rebuildable active-state index).

Option A — frontmatter parser implementation (dependency or dependency-free) is the generator's call; round-trip success criteria pin behavior. Cited: package.json lines 64-75 (no YAML dep present, only sax).

Autonomous self-approval. Every assumption cites evidence from the locked research doc (.bober/research/research-20260627-knowledge-platform-landscape.md §3b/§4) and existing substrate: FactStore src/state/facts.ts, reconcile src/orchestrator/memory/reconcile.ts, ExternalMcpServer src/mcp/external-client.ts, ObservabilityProvider config src/config/schema.ts:298-309, CLI pattern src/cli/commands/medical.ts + facts.ts, namespace resolver src/state/memory.ts:27 + src/teams/registry.ts loadTeam.

assumptions: The vault layer is domain-agnostic: medical is the first consumer but no medical-specific symbols, paths, or frontmatter keys are hardcoded. Evidence: brief SCOPE 'Keep this DOMAIN-AGNOSTIC'; medical store kept separate at src/medical/health-store.ts.

FactStore is the derived index; markdown+frontmatter is canonical. Reindex reuses writeFact/reconcileFact at ingest rather than introducing a new persistence path. Evidence: src/orchestrator/memory/reconcile.ts:148-154 (writeFact wrapper), research §3b lines 150-152.
Generic frontmatter->fact mapping: scope=domain, subject=frontmatter `id` else vault-relative note path, predicate=frontmatter key, value=stringified value (Q1).
Notes with frontmatter `status: superseded` contribute no active facts during reindex; git history is the bitemporal source-of-truth (Q3, research §3b lines 152-156).

The Obsidian MCP adapter is on-device only and server-agnostic: the MCP server is declared in config (mcpCommand/mcpArgs/mcpEnv) mirroring ObservabilityProvider, wrapped via ExternalMcpServer; tool names are configurable with documented cyanheads/obsidian-mcp-server defaults (Q2). Evidence: src/mcp/external-client.ts, src/config/schema.ts:298-309.

All wall-clock timestamps are injected at the CLI boundary, never read inside store/reindex/mapping code, matching the FactStore purity contract. Evidence: src/state/facts.ts:130-135, src/cli/commands/facts.ts:86.

CLI handlers never throw: on error they write a chalk.red message and set process.exitCode=1, and always close the store in a finally block. Evidence: src/cli/commands/facts.ts:135-142, src/cli/commands/medical.ts:111-120.

The derived FactStore lives at the team/namespace memory path resolved via loadTeam(config).memoryNamespace -> factsDbPath, not a new ad-hoc location. Evidence: src/cli/commands/facts.ts:41-50,99, src/state/memory.ts:27.

profile.yaml is a hook only: this spec leaves a recognized profile.yaml location and a resolver that detects (but never decrypts) a SOPS-encrypted profile. SOPS encryption policy is out of scope (owned by a sibling spec). Evidence: brief OUT clause + research §3b lines 155-156.
Binary attachments (scan PDFs) stay OUT of git; the layer documents and scaffolds an attachments-dir convention rather than storing binaries. Evidence: research §3b line 157.
outOfScope: Medical-specific ingest (lab-PDF parsing, Apple Health, WHOOP) — owned by spec-20260628-medical-ingest
Medical analysis / proactive review / recommendation judge-loop — owned by spec-20260628-medical-analysis
The unified priority hub, task inbox, and cross-repo collector — owned by the hub spec
Implementing SOPS encryption/decryption policy or key management beyond leaving a profile.yaml hook
Semantic vault search (Nooscope) and FTS over note bodies
Migrating existing medical health.db rows into the vault format
Git commit/branch automation for the vault (humans/Obsidian/sibling tooling commit)

features.title: Vault note model + frontmatter round-trip I/O
features.description: A typed VaultNote model with pure functions to parse a markdown file's YAML frontmatter and body into a typed object and serialize back, plus filesystem read/write/list helpers over a vault directory. Domain-agnostic; documents Dataview-compatible frontmatter conventions.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: parseNote on a fixture note with string, number, ISO-date, list, and status frontmatter keys yields a VaultNote whose frontmatter object preserves each value's parsed type and whose body equals the markdown after the closing delimiter.
AC2: serializeNote(parseNote(x)) re-parses to a frontmatter object deep-equal to the original for the documented Dataview conventions (round-trip fidelity).
AC3: listNotes(dir) returns every .md file under the vault directory (recursively) and readNote/writeNote round-trip a note to disk.

features.title: Derived FactStore index over note frontmatter (reconcile-at-ingest)
features.description: A pure mapping from a VaultNote's frontmatter to FactInput records and a reindex function that applies them to a FactStore via the existing writeFact/reconcileFact path, honoring status:superseded notes. The FactStore becomes a derived, rebuildable index over canonical note frontmatter.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: noteToFacts maps a note to one FactInput per frontmatter key with scope=domain, subject=frontmatter id else vault-relative path, predicate=key, value=stringified value.
AC2: reindexNotes run twice over identical notes reports the second pass as all-noop (no duplicate active facts), proving reconcile dedup at ingest.
AC3: a note whose frontmatter value changes between passes results in the prior fact superseded (t_invalidated set) and the new value active.
AC4: a note with frontmatter status:superseded contributes zero active facts to the store.

features.title: `bober vault reindex` CLI command
features.description: A domain-agnostic CLI command that walks a vault directory, parses every note's frontmatter, and rebuilds the derived FactStore at the resolved team/namespace memory path, printing per-run counts. Reuses note-io, the reindex mapping, factsDbPath/ensureFactsDir, and loadTeam namespace resolution.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `bober vault reindex --scope <domain> [--vault <dir>]` is registered and listed in `bober vault --help`.
AC2: running the command against a fixture vault populates facts.db at the namespace path with the expected active facts and exits 0 printing notesParsed/factsAdded/factsSuperseded counts.
AC3: on a missing/invalid vault directory the handler writes a red error to stderr and sets exit code 1 without throwing, and always closes the FactStore.

features.title: On-device Obsidian MCP read/write adapter + config
features.description: A config schema entry declaring an on-device Obsidian MCP server (mirroring ObservabilityProvider) and a thin VaultMcpAdapter that wraps ExternalMcpServer to read, write, and list notes through configurable tool names, with an on-device-only guard rejecting non-local server declarations.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: a VaultSection config with an obsidian MCP server (mcpCommand/mcpArgs/mcpEnv + optional tool-name overrides) parses and round-trips through the config schema.
AC2: VaultMcpAdapter.readNote/writeNote/listNotes invoke the configured (or default cyanheads) tool names on an injected ExternalMcpServer and return parsed VaultNote results.
AC3: the on-device guard rejects a server declaration whose transport implies a non-local network endpoint, surfacing a clear error and never spawning the server.

features.title: Vault profile.yaml hook + Dataview/attachments conventions
features.description: A resolver that recognizes a profile.yaml at the vault root, parsing it when plaintext and detecting (without decrypting) a SOPS-encrypted profile, plus exported convention constants for Dataview frontmatter keys and the gitignored attachments directory. Leaves a hook for later SOPS wiring; implements no encryption.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: resolveProfile reads a plaintext vault/profile.yaml into a typed VaultProfile.
AC2: resolveProfile detects a SOPS-encrypted profile.yaml (sops markers present) and returns an opaque {encrypted:true} marker without decrypting or reading secret values.
AC3: exported convention constants document the canonical frontmatter status values and the attachments directory name, and a missing profile.yaml returns undefined rather than throwing.

integrationPoints: FactStore + writeFact/reconcileFact (src/state/facts.ts, src/orchestrator/memory/reconcile.ts) — derived index + reconcile-at-ingest
TeamConfigSchema + memoryDir + loadTeam (src/config/schema.ts:361, src/state/memory.ts:27, src/teams/registry.ts) — namespace-scoped derived store path
CLI command tree (src/cli/index.ts + src/cli/commands/*.ts) — `bober vault` registration
techNotes.dataModel: Canonical: markdown notes with YAML frontmatter inside an Obsidian vault directory. Derived: FactStore semantic_facts rows where scope=domain, subject=note identity, predicate=frontmatter key, value=stringified frontmatter value; active rows == active notes' fields. Git history is the bitemporal source-of-truth.
securityConsiderations: Obsidian MCP adapter is on-device only — non-local server declarations are rejected before spawn.
MCP env (mcpEnv) may contain tokens — never log it; reuse ExternalMcpServer's sanitization (src/mcp/external-client.ts:66-77).
profile.yaml SOPS detection must never decrypt or surface encrypted values.
Binary attachments stay out of git (attachments dir convention + gitignore hint).

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: All new code lives under a new src/vault/ module plus one CLI command file and additive config-schema/CLI-index edits; no medical-specific symbols are introduced into the vault module.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Grep src/vault/ for medical-specific identifiers returns none; typecheck and build pass.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Mapping, reindex, and store code never read the wall clock; every timestamp is an injected parameter, matching the FactStore purity contract.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Grep src/vault/ for Date.now( and new Date( returns no occurrences outside the CLI handler boundary; unit tests inject now.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The Obsidian MCP adapter refuses non-local server declarations and the profile resolver never decrypts SOPS-encrypted content.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit tests assert the on-device guard rejection and the SOPS-opaque return path.

constraints: Reuse existing substrate (FactStore, writeFact/reconcileFact, ExternalMcpServer, TeamConfigSchema, memoryDir/loadTeam, CLI command pattern) — do not reimplement persistence, reconcile, or MCP transport.
Domain-agnostic: no hardcoded medical keys/paths in the vault module.
CLI handlers never throw (set process.exitCode=1) and always close stores in finally.
Each sprint must build (tsc) and pass the existing test suite plus its new tests.
